name: Apple-Walnut Upside-Down Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. In a 9-inch pie pan, combine brown sugar, melted margarine or butter, and chopped walnuts.
3. Carefully place one crust on top of the sugar/nut mixture; trim even with the edge of the pie pan.
4. Combine remaining ingredients (remaining apples, flour, lemon juice, and cinnamon) and put into the pie crust.
5. Cover with the remaining pie crust; trim even with the edge of the pie pan.
6. Fold the crust edge inward toward the center of the pie; seal edges lightly with water.
7. Prick the top of the crust to vent steam; place on a foil-covered baking sheet.
8. Bake for 40-45 minutes or until golden brown. Let stand for 2 minutes.
9. Carefully run a knife tip around the edge of the plate to loosen the pie.
10. Invert onto a serving platter. Serve warm with ice cream or whipped cream.
